input.description: Check existence of test and bundle

data.output:
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"os"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"testing"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/testfixtures"
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
14		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
15		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
16	)
17	
18	// TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigCLI is the SD-024 Stage 1 behavioral
19	// proof that the CLI dispatch path at runAgentExecuteLoop carries
20	// review_max_retries from .ddx/config.yaml through to the running loop.
21	//
22	// The test mirrors the production wiring introduced in agent_cmd.go:
23	// it calls config.LoadAndResolve against a real on-disk .ddx/config.yaml
24	// (the same call the CLI dispatch site issues) and then invokes
25	// ExecuteBeadWorker.RunWithConfig with an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime shaped
26	// identically to the runtime the CLI builds. The deterministic
27	// review-failure fixture from testfixtures provides the executor +
28	// reviewer pair so behavior is observable end-to-end without a real
29	// agent harness.
30	//
31	// Configured values:
32	//   - .ddx/config.yaml: review_max_retries: 5
33	//   - fixture: FailUntilCall=4 (attempts 1-4 return reviewer error,
34	//     attempt 5 returns APPROVE)
35	//
36	// Expected behavior:
37	//   - 5 RunWithConfig iterations drive 5 reviewer attempts.
38	//   - attempts 1-4 emit review-error events.
39	//   - attempt 5 emits an APPROVE review event and the loop closes
40	//     the bead via CloseWithEvidence.
41	//   - no review-manual-required event fires (threshold=5 ≥ attempts=5).
42	func TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigCLI(t *testing.T) {
43		const (
44			failUntil   = 4
45			threshold   = 5
46			beadID      = "ddx-cli-rmr-001"
47			fixedRev    = "cafebabe00112233"
48			assigneeStr = "cli-e2e-worker"
49		)
50	
51		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
52		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
53		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
54	
55		// Real on-disk .ddx/config.yaml — this is the file the CLI dispatch
56		// path's config.LoadAndResolve call reads. The presence of
57		// review_max_retries: 5 here is the entire premise of the test.
58		cfgYAML := `version: "1.0"
59	library:
60	  path: ./library
61	  repository:
62	    url: https://github.com/example/repo
63	    branch: main
64	review_max_retries: 5
65	`
66		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(cfgYAML), 0o644))
67	
68		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
69		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
70		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{ID: beadID, Title: "cli e2e review-retry threshold", Priority: 0}))
71	
72		runner := &testfixtures.ReviewFailureRunner{
73			ResultRev:     fixedRev,
74			FailUntilCall: failUntil,
75		}
76	
77		worker := &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
78			Store:    store,
79			Executor: runner.Executor(),
80			Reviewer: runner.Reviewer(),
81		}
82	
83		// Same shape the migrated CLI dispatch site builds.
84		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{Assignee: assigneeStr}
85		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
86		require.NoError(t, err)
87		require.Equal(t, threshold, rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries(),
88			"LoadAndResolve must surface review_max_retries from .ddx/config.yaml")
89	
90		runtime := agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
91			Once:        true,
92			ProjectRoot: t.TempDir(), // execute-bead worktree base; isolated from project root.
93		}
94	
95		// Drive failUntil + 1 iterations (one --once invocation per attempt,
96		// matching the production loop's per-iteration claim/release rhythm).
97		const totalIterations = failUntil + 1
98		for i := 0; i < totalIterations; i++ {
99			_, runErr := worker.RunWithConfig(context.Background(), rcfg, runtime)
100			require.NoErrorf(t, runErr, "iteration %d: RunWithConfig", i+1)
101			if i < totalIterations-1 {
102				// On a reviewer-error iteration the loop intentionally leaves
103				// the bead claimed; production re-picks it up next poll. The
104				// test drives that re-pickup explicitly via Unclaim.
105				require.NoErrorf(t, store.Unclaim(beadID), "iteration %d: unclaim", i+1)
106			}
107		}
108	
109		assert.Equal(t, totalIterations, runner.ReviewCalls(),
110			"reviewer must be invoked once per driven iteration")
111		assert.Equal(t, totalIterations, runner.ExecCalls(),
112			"executor must be invoked once per driven iteration")
113	
114		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
115		require.NoError(t, err)
116	
117		var (
118			reviewErrorCount    int
119			reviewApproveCount  int
120			manualRequiredCount int
121		)
122		for _, ev := range events {
123			switch ev.Kind {
124			case "review-error":
125				reviewErrorCount++
126			case "review":
127				if ev.Summary == "APPROVE" {
128					reviewApproveCount++
129				}
130			case "review-manual-required":
131				manualRequiredCount++
132			}
133		}
134	
135		assert.Equal(t, failUntil, reviewErrorCount,
136			"reviewer-error count must match the fixture's FailUntilCall")
137		assert.Equal(t, 1, reviewApproveCount,
138			"the (FailUntilCall+1)th iteration must record an APPROVE review event")
139		assert.Equal(t, 0, manualRequiredCount,
140			"with review_max_retries=%d ≥ attempts=%d the loop must NOT emit review-manual-required",
141			threshold, totalIterations)
142	
143		got, err := store.Get(beadID)
144		require.NoError(t, err)
145		assert.Equal(t, "closed", got.Status,
146			"after APPROVE on attempt %d the loop must close the bead", totalIterations)
147	
148		// Defensive: a stale heartbeat ticker in the loop could outlive the
149		// final iteration. Give it a beat to settle so test cleanup is clean.
150		time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)
151	}
152

data.text: The migration and test are already in place from a prior merged attempt. The dispatch site at `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:2020-2052` uses `config.LoadAndResolve` + `worker.RunWithConfig` with error handling, and the e2e test passes. Writing a no-changes rationale.

input.content:
Bead ddx-dd7fce7e — no changes needed.

What is done:
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:2014-2052 already wires the runAgentExecuteLoop CLI dispatch site
  through config.LoadAndResolve + ExecuteBeadWorker.RunWithConfig. The LoadAndResolve
  error is captured and returned (`return fmt.Errorf("load resolved config: %w", err)`),
  satisfying the bead notes' requirement to surface the error rather than swallow it
  into a zero-value ResolvedConfig.
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_e2e_test.go contains TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigCLI,
  the SD-024 Stage 1 behavioral proof. It writes a real .ddx/config.yaml with
  review_max_retries: 5, calls config.LoadAndResolve, and drives ExecuteBeadWorker.RunWithConfig
  through 5 iterations of the deterministic ReviewFailureRunner fixture (FailUntilCall=4),
  asserting: 4 review-error events, 1 APPROVE review, 0 review-manual-required, and
  final bead status == "closed".

Verification performed in this worktree:
- `cd cli && go test -run TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigCLI ./cmd/...` -> PASS (0.033s).

Origin of the existing work:
- git log shows commit 1ceb2a31 "fix(cmd/work): surface LoadAndResolve error in CLI dispatch
  [ddx-dd7fce7e]" merged via 3067bd79 prior to this attempt's checkpoint (9e0043c0).
  The prior attempt's changes are present on the base of this worktree.

Why no commit:
- All AC items (the targeted test passes; the dispatch site is migrated; the LoadAndResolve
  error is surfaced) are already satisfied by the merged prior attempt. There is no code
  change to make. Per execute-bead instructions, a justified no_changes is preferred over
  a synthetic or red commit.

Follow-up needs:
- None for this bead. If the bead was reopened on review grounds, the review-findings
  section was not included in this prompt; without specific findings there is no
  actionable delta to apply. If a downstream review identifies a concrete gap, surface
  the findings via <review-findings/> on the next attempt.

data.text:
Bead work is already present from a prior merged attempt (commit `1ceb2a31`):
- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:2020-2052` — LoadAndResolve + RunWithConfig migration with error surfacing.
- `cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_e2e_test.go` — `TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigCLI` passes (`go test -run ... ./cmd/...` → ok 0.033s).

No code change needed. Wrote `.ddx/executions/20260425T215833-b68e06fc/no_changes_rationale.txt` documenting the situation.
